doc.id: Potkay and Feng - 2023 - Do stomata optimize turgor-driven growth A new framework for integrating stomata response with whol_appendix.pdf
doc.title: Do stomata optimize turgor-driven growth? (Supporting Information: Notes S1 – Full model, parameter estimation, simulations)

model_card.name: S4 Leaf and canopy-level carbon assimilation
task: compute canopy-mean net assimilation An using a big-leaf hyperbolic-minimum formulation
compute RuBP carboxylation-limited (Ac) and electron-transport-limited (Aj) rates
compute electron transport rate J via hyperbolic minimum (Jmax vs light-limited JI)
derive marginal carbon profit of water λ = dAn/dE needed for stomatal optimization

assumptions.text: A big-leaf solution is used to scale leaf-level photosynthesis to canopy-mean assimilation (noting the approach has limitations).

evidence.snippet: For simplicity, we here apply a big-leaf solution to scale up for canopy-level carbon assimilation, despite the approach’s limitations...

assumptions.text: Net assimilation An is represented as the hyperbolic minimum of carboxylation-limited and electron-transport-limited rates (Ac and Aj), smoothed by curvature parameter θc.

evidence.snippet: The big-leaf solution method applies a hyperbolic minimum of a RuBP carboxylation-limited and an electron transport-limited net rates, Aj and Ac...

assumptions.text: Electron transport rate J is represented as a hyperbolic minimum of biochemical maximum Jmax and light-limited JI=ϰ·I, with curvature θJ fixed at 0.90.

evidence.snippet: The electron transport rate, J, is given by the hyperbolic minimum of ... Jmax and JI ... where JI = ϰ·I ... The smoothness ... θJ = 0.90.

assumptions.text: For temperature sensitivity ξ, only temperature-dependences of Vc,max, Jmax, and Rd are considered.

evidence.snippet: Similarly, we decompose ξ into its components, noting that we only consider here the temperature-dependences of Vc,max, Jmax, and Rd...

equations.explanation: Supply-limited CO2 diffusion definition of net assimilation.

evidence.snippet: An = gc(ca − ci) (S4.1)

equations.explanation: Demand-limited biochemical uptake as hyperbolic minimum of Aj and Ac.

evidence.snippet: An = (Aj + Ac)/(2θc) − sqrt(((Aj + Ac)/(2θc))^2 − AjAc/θc) (S4.2)

equations.explanation: Quadratic form stated for the hyperbolic-minimum definition.

evidence.snippet: i.e., θcAn^2 – (Ac + Aj)An + AcAj = 0

equations.explanation: RuBP carboxylation-limited net rate (Farquhar-type).

evidence.snippet: Ac = Vc,max (ci − Γ*)/(ci + Kc(1 + oa/Ko)) − Rd (S4.3)

equations.explanation: Electron-transport-limited net rate (Farquhar-type).
Temperature dependence of Rd captured via proportionality with Vc,max at 25°C and a Q10 relationship (as stated).

evidence.snippet: Q10 relationship (Q10 = 2; Rd = 0.01 · Vc,max,25°C · Q10^((TL − 25)/10).

equations.selection_reason: TL is defined elsewhere in K; this relation is stated with 25°C. Temperature-unit convention is not fully specified within this chunk.

equations.explanation: Light-limited electron transport rate is proportional to absorbed insolation.

evidence.snippet: light-limited rate ... JI ... where JI = ϰ·I, I is the leaf area-averaged solar insolation...

equations.explanation: Electron transport rate as hyperbolic minimum of Jmax and JI.

evidence.snippet: J = (Jmax + JI)/(2θJ) − sqrt(((Jmax + JI)/(2θJ))^2 − Jmax JI/θJ) (S4.5)

equations.explanation: Marginal carbon profit of water expressed via derivatives with respect to conductances.

evidence.snippet: λ = dAn/dE = (dAn/dgc) / ((dE/dgw)(dgw/dgc)) (S4.6)

equations.explanation: Two equivalent expressions for dAn/dgc (supply-based vs biochemical demand curve), introducing k and ξ.

evidence.snippet: dAn/dgc = ∂An/∂gc + ∂An/∂ci · dci/dgc = k·dci/dgc + ξ·dTL/dgw · dgw/dgc (S4.7)

equations.explanation: Supply-side partial derivatives evaluated using An=gc(ca−ci), yielding an equation to solve for dci/dgc.

evidence.snippet: dAn/dgc = An/gc − gc·dci/dgc = k·dci/dgc + ξ·dTL/dgw·dgw/dgc (S4.8)

equations.explanation: Rearranged solution for dci/dgc.

evidence.snippet: dci/dgc = 1/(k+gc) (An/gc − ξ·dTL/dgw·dgw/dgc) (S4.9)

equations.explanation: Closed-form expression for dAn/dgc after substituting dci/dgc.

evidence.snippet: dAn/dgc = k/(k+gc)·An/gc + ξ·gc/(k+gc)·dTL/dgw·dgw/dgc (S4.10)

equations.explanation: Final closed-form definition of λ combining Eq. S4.6 with Eq. S4.10 and Eq. S3.4 (as stated).

evidence.snippet: λ = k/(k+gc) · An/(gc·(dgw/dgc)·(dE/dgw)) − gc/(k+gc) · ξL/(4εσTL^3 + Cp gb) (S4.11)

equations.latex: k = \frac{\partial A_n}{\partial c_i} = \frac{\partial A_n}{\partial A_c}\frac{\partial A_c}{\partial c_i} + \frac{\partial A_n}{\partial A_j}\frac{\partial A_j}{\partial c_i}
equations.explanation: Decomposition of biochemical demand-curve slope k into Ac and Aj components (big-leaf).

evidence.snippet: k = ∂An/∂ci = (∂An/∂Ac)(∂Ac/∂ci) + (∂An/∂Aj)(∂Aj/∂ci) (S4.12)

equations.explanation: Derivative of An with respect to Ac implied by the hyperbolic-minimum formulation.

evidence.snippet: ∂An/∂Ac = (1/(2θc))[1 − (Ac + (1−2θc)Aj)/sqrt((Aj+Ac)^2 − 4θc Aj Ac)] (S4.13)

equations.explanation: Derivative of Ac with respect to ci from Eq. S4.3 (as provided).

evidence.snippet: ∂Ac/∂ci = Vc,max [Γ* + Kc(1 + oa/Ko)] / [ci + Kc(1 + oa/Ko)]^2 (S4.14)

equations.explanation: Derivative of An with respect to Aj implied by the hyperbolic-minimum formulation.

evidence.snippet: ∂An/∂Aj = (1/(2θc))[1 − (Aj + (1−2θc)Ac)/sqrt((Aj+Ac)^2 − 4θc Aj Ac)] (S4.15)

equations.explanation: Derivative of Aj with respect to ci from Eq. S4.4 (as provided).

equations.latex: \xi = \frac{\partial A_n}{\partial T_L} = \frac{\partial A_n}{\partial A_c}\frac{\partial A_c}{\partial T_L} + \frac{\partial A_n}{\partial A_j}\frac{\partial A_j}{\partial T_L} = \frac{\partial A_n}{\partial A_c}\left(\frac{\partial A_c}{\partial V_{c,\max}}\frac{\partial V_{c,\max}}{\partial T_L} + \frac{\partial A_c}{\partial R_d}\frac{\partial R_d}{\partial T_L}\right) + \frac{\partial A_n}{\partial A_j}\left(\frac{\partial A_j}{\partial J}\frac{\partial J}{\partial J_{\max}}\frac{\partial J_{\max}}{\partial T_L} + \frac{\partial A_j}{\partial R_d}\frac{\partial R_d}{\partial T_L}\right)
equations.explanation: Decomposition of ξ into components from temperature dependence of Vc,max, Jmax, and Rd (as stated).

equations.latex: \xi = \frac{\partial A_n}{\partial A_c}\left(\frac{A_c+R_d}{V_{c,\max}}\frac{\partial V_{c,\max}}{\partial T_L} - \frac{\partial R_d}{\partial T_L}\right) + \frac{\partial A_n}{\partial A_j}\left(\frac{A_j+R_d}{J}\frac{\partial J}{\partial J_{\max}}\frac{\partial J_{\max}}{\partial T_L} - \frac{\partial R_d}{\partial T_L}\right)
equations.explanation: Simplified expression for ξ (as provided).

evidence.snippet: which simplifies further to ... (S4.18)

parameters.estimation: Approximate value stated for quantum yield 0.3 mol photon·(mol e-)-1 and average PAR wavelength 550 nm.

evidence.snippet: ϰ ≈ 6.90·10-7 mol e-·J-1 ... quantum yield ... average PAR wavelength of 550 nm
Q10 relationship (Q10 = 2; Rd = 0.01 · Vc,max,25°C · Q10^((TL - 25)/10).

parameters.name: Rd_scale_factor
parameters.estimation: Stated proportionality factor linking Rd to Vc,max,25°C.

steps: Compute JI = κ·I and then J via the hyperbolic-minimum relation (Eq. S4.5).
Compute Ac(ci) (Eq. S4.3) and Aj(ci) (Eq. S4.4).
Compute biochemical demand An_demand(ci) using hyperbolic minimum of Aj and Ac (Eq. S4.2).
Compute diffusion supply An_supply(ci) = gc(ca − ci) (Eq. S4.1).
Solve for ci such that An_supply(ci) = An_demand(ci); set An = An_supply = An_demand.
Compute ξ using Eq. S4.17–S4.18 (requires ∂Vc,max/∂TL, ∂Jmax/∂TL, ∂Rd/∂TL, and ∂J/∂Jmax).
Compute λ using Eq. S4.11 together with dE/dgw and dgw/dgc (referenced to Eq. S3.8 & S3.10).

evidence.snippet: The net assimilation rate, An ... is given by two simultaneous expressions...
final closed form definition of the marginal carbon profit of water ... (S4.11)

unresolved_assets.label: Derivatives needed for ξ

evidence.snippet: ξ = ... (Ac+Rd)/Vc,max · ∂Vc,max/∂TL ... (Aj+Rd)/J · (∂J/∂Jmax)(∂Jmax/∂TL) ... − ∂Rd/∂TL (S4.18)

open_questions.question: What is the explicit functional form for Vc,max(TL) (referenced as Harley & Baldocchi, 1995; Eq. 1) and its parameter values used here?

open_questions.likely_location: Harley & Baldocchi (1995) Eq. 1 or elsewhere in the SI/methods where parameters are specified.
